title: hydrangea macrophylla f. pubescens
intro: Hydrangea macrophylla f. pubescens, also known as the bigleaf hydrangea or hairy-leaved bigleaf hydrangea, is a shrub native to Japan and Korea, characterized by its large, showy flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Hydrangea macrophylla f. pubescens include reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
